codeReview.summary: The fix adequately addresses the prior blocking scrutiny failure. `CodexClient.initialize()` now coalesces callers onto a single in-flight handshake and caches the successful initialize result, while `reconnect()` reuses that state instead of issuing a second `initialize` against the same subprocess. The added client- and hook-level regressions cover both concurrent initialize/reconnect and send-before-startup-settles, and the worker transcript/handoff shows a delayed-start PTY check where the first post-launch action completed successfully without an initialize error. I did not identify a new high-confidence correctness issue in the changed code.

summary: Reviewed the prior failed feature, prior scrutiny report, fix handoff, transcript skeleton, skill guidance, and commit `760165a7c14a5109ee84e2fcb644af0dcd236dd8`. The fix resolves the original Codex initialize race by reusing one startup handshake/result across initialize and reconnect paths, and I found no new high-confidence issues in the fix diff.
